slice: Add a repository-root corpus test that validates the committed continuity index plus additive records and rejects prose or missing targets in machine-relation arrays.

contracts: the actual committed index and additive records pass the aggregate validator
CM-1000, CM-1001, and CM-1002 remain present
every contradicts and supersedes target matches CM-####
every machine-relation target resolves within the loaded corpus

claim_states.observed: Synthetic aggregate-validator tests existed, and M-005 explicitly handed off a corpus-level migration test. The committed corpus test did not exist before this slice.
claim_states.inferred: A repository-root fixture closes the gap between validator behavior on synthetic examples and preservation of the actual migrated continuity graph.
claim_states.proposed: Run this test in CI at an exact commit and retain the raw Node test report as immutable workflow evidence.
claim_states.superseded: The prior test posture in which only synthetic fixtures guarded relation integrity.
claim_states.unresolved: No workflow run or observed Node execution exists for commit 87edbc839cbafc621684e08ebfd396ed544f4434. Source readback proves the contract is committed, not that it passes in CI.

verification.claim_limit: Do not claim corpus validity or CI passage until the test executes successfully in a retained runtime.

privacy_boundary: Only public repository files, abstract continuity records, and commit metadata were inspected. No private chat, health, credential, identifying personal, or unreviewed uploaded-file content was published.
falsification_route: Fetch the exact test blob and run node --test tools/continuity/continuity-corpus.test.mjs in a clean checkout of commit 87edbc839cbafc621684e08ebfd396ed544f4434. Falsify the source-contract claim if the file does not load the repository corpus or does not reject non-ID or missing relation targets. Falsify runtime validity if either test fails.
